serving.natural_language_portion: A standard serving of kidney bean rolls is 6 pieces, approximately 120 g, typically consumed as a dessert or snack.

nutrient_source.title: Kidney Bean Rolls (Yun Dou Juan) — estimated from USDA FoodData Central: White Kidney Beans (cooked) and Sugar, with composition adjustment for sweet bean confection
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central (Foundation Foods / SR Legacy)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No direct entry for kidney bean rolls exists in HPB Singapore Foodo, USDA FoodData Central, or FAO/INFOODS SEA databases. Nutrient values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central data for cooked white kidney beans (FDC ID 175200) and granulated sugar, weighted to reflect the typical formulation of kidney bean rolls: approximately 70% white kidney bean paste and 30% added sugar, with negligible fat. Energy calculated using Atwater factors. Values represent a reasonable central estimate for this traditional Chinese confection.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — kidney beans / white beans (boiled)
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database; Atkinson FS, Brand-Miller JC, Foster-Powell K, Buyken AE, Goletzke J. Am J Clin Nutr. 2021
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for kidney bean rolls (Yun Dou Juan) as a finished confection. GI of 29 is derived from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for boiled white kidney beans / cannellini beans (GI ~29–31, low category), which form approximately 70% of the product by weight. The addition of sugar raises the effective GI modestly; however, the high fibre and protein content of the bean base and the relatively small sugar fraction are expected to keep the overall GI in the low range. GL per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (29 × 54.0) / 100 ≈ 15.7, rounded to 15.8 for 120 g serving (45.0 g carbohydrate per 100 g × 1.2 = 54.0 g).

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium content (~15 mg per 100 g) and high potassium (~310 mg per 100 g)
hypertension.guidance: Kidney bean rolls are very low in sodium and provide meaningful potassium, making them a suitable occasional dessert for individuals managing hypertension, provided portion size is controlled to limit total sugar intake.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic load (~15.8 per 6-piece serving) and significant added sugar (~22 g per 100 g)
type2_diabetes.guidance: Although the bean base confers a low GI (~29) due to its high fibre and protein content, the added sugar in kidney bean rolls produces a moderate glycemic load per serving; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should limit portion size to 2–3 pieces and monitor postprandial glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Negligible saturated fat, zero cholesterol, and high dietary fibre (~4.5 g per 100 g) from kidney beans
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Kidney bean rolls are very low in saturated fat and cholesterol-free, and the soluble fibre from kidney beans may contribute to modest LDL-cholesterol reduction; they represent a heart-friendly dessert option when consumed in moderate portions.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Kidney Bean Rolls (芸豆卷, Yún Dòu Juǎn) are a traditional Chinese palace dessert originating from the imperial kitchens of the Qing Dynasty in Beijing. The confection is made by steaming white kidney beans (cannellini beans), mashing them into a smooth paste, and rolling the paste into a thin sheet. A sweet filling — typically red bean paste or sweetened green bean paste — is spread across the sheet, which is then tightly rolled and sliced into bite-sized cylinders. The result is a delicate, mildly sweet roll with a soft, smooth, slightly dense texture and a subtle earthy bean flavour. Kidney bean rolls are naturally gluten-free, vegan, and very low in fat. They provide moderate protein (~6.5 g per 100 g) and meaningful dietary fibre (~4.5 g per 100 g) from the bean base, along with useful amounts of iron, folate, potassium, and magnesium. The glycemic index is low (~29) due to the high fibre and protein content of the kidney bean paste, though the added sugar contributes a moderate glycemic load per standard serving of 6 pieces (~120 g). Clinically, kidney bean rolls are suitable for most dietary patterns as an occasional dessert; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should moderate portion size due to the added sugar content. The dish is a staple of Beijing snack culture and is commonly served at traditional teahouses, dim sum restaurants, and as a festival treat. In Singapore, it is offered at Chinese restaurants specialising in northern Chinese or palace-style cuisine.
common_user_queries: Are kidney bean rolls gluten free?
How many calories in kidney bean rolls?

What is 芸豆卷 made of?
Traditional Chinese palace dessert bean roll

confidence_reason: No exact entry for kidney bean rolls exists in any approved primary nutrient database (HPB Singapore Foodo, USDA FoodData Central, FAO/INFOODS SEA, ICMR-NIN). Nutrient values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central data for cooked white kidney beans and sugar, weighted by typical formulation. GI is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for boiled white kidney beans as the closest analogous food. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central (cooked white kidney beans, FDC ID 175200, and granulated sugar) weighted at approximately 70:30 bean-to-sugar ratio. No direct laboratory analysis of this finished product was available in any approved source at time of processing. 2. vitamin_a_mcg_rae is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FoodData Central entry for cooked white kidney beans and is not expected to be a significant contributor in this product. 3. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database for this food type and is not expected to be present in a plant-based bean confection. 4. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FoodData Central entry for cooked white kidney beans at the level of detail available. 5. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the primary source data used for this estimation. 6. vitamin_b12_mcg is 0.0 as this is a fully plant-based product containing no animal-derived ingredients; vitamin B12 is naturally zero in plant foods. 7. trans_fat_g is 0.0 as this product contains no hydrogenated oils or animal fats; confirmed zero by formulation. 8. cholesterol_mg is 0.0 as this is a fully plant-based product; cholesterol is naturally zero. 9. GI estimated from University of Sydney GI Database entry for boiled white kidney beans (GI ~29); see gi_source for full estimation rationale. 10. The input food_name '宫廷芸豆卷 Kidney Bean Rolls' has been normalised to canonical English 'Kidney Bean Rolls'; the Chinese prefix '宫廷' (palace-style) is preserved in aliases and description. 11. Serving size of 120 g (6 pieces) is estimated based on typical portion weight for this confection; no manufacturer weight data was available from the field survey listing.
